Bags I almost bought in Japan 🇯🇵

Vintage luxury shopping in Osaka is dangerous in the best way. The prices were tempting. The selection was unreal. And yes… some of these were real deals.

But I’m learning something important:

A good price is not the same thing as the right purchase.

Building a fine jewelry and luxury collection on purpose means walking away sometimes — even when you can afford it.

I’d rather regret not buying it than regret buying it for the wrong reason.

Which one would you have taken home?

And more importantly… are you a deal hunter or a disciplined collector?

Save this if you’re building your collection intentionally.

This weekend, I went vintage shopping for an Hermes Birkin bag in the most unexpected place: DISNEYLAND! 

On Main Street between the Penny Arcade and the Fortuosity Shop, the New Century Jewelry Shop in Disneyland sells vintage designer handbags from @whatgoesaroundnyc. I’ve spotted Gucci, Chanel and Louis Vuitton before but this was the first time I’ve spotted a Birkin!

On my last visit, they had a 35cm Hermes Birkin from 1997 in pristine condition for $19,500. 

Who knew Disneyland sold Birkin bags! What do you think? Would you buy a luxury handbag at an amusement park?

The Hermès deal that almost got me 😭

I really thought I found the one a Birkin priced way under what I’d been seeing everywhere else in Japan. Too good to be true… and it kind of was.

When you’re luxury shopping secondhand, especially in places like Japan, you have to read every detail carefully. Some bags look perfect at first glance, but the fine print matters more than the price tag.

This one taught me a lot about what to look for, what questions to ask, and why patience is part of the process when you’re hunting for your dream bag.

Still grateful for the experience and honestly, it made finding the right one even sweeter ✨

Addressing the allegation that Hermes makes their bags in China in this last segment of my 3-part Reels video.  This is a flat-out LIE, and that part of the viral video was really meant to mislead people. And sadly so many believed it because let's be honest, it's a good excuse to justify and validate buying fakes without feeling guilty (and for lower prices) 💀 But that piece of information shared was really meant to misinform, and was just a ploy to convince customers to buy their counterfeit Hermes bags ✌🏻 

The reality is, as of my post today, Hermes is the only remaining widely-known luxury maison that 100% manufactures their bags (especially the Birkin, Kelly, Constance) in France. There are other smaller, independent brands that do (Joseph Duclos is an independent luxury brand that also manufacures all their bags 100% in France). Sad that the other mainstream "luxury" brands no longer do this because they stand to make bigger margins if they produce elsewhere (France has very high labor costs for highly-skilled artisans).

The crazy prices these mainstream designer brands command today factor in the money they invested in prime real estate locations of their boutiques, as well as a lot of marketing and communications to continue elevating & maintaining their brand value (hiring top celebrities as "ambassadors" to front campaigns, giving out free bags to influencers/press, social and traditional media ad spend, traveling fashion shows, etc). But they sadly cut costs on materials and labor. There is a lack of transparency in their supply chains despite their efforts to publicly pledge their commitments for "sustainability" and manufacturing products in "greener" ways (it's all just PR) 💀

So the bottomline is this: if you plan on buying a very costly bag from any of these "offending" mainstream/popular designer brands that are categorized as "luxury,", think long and hard before purchasing. These brands really need to do better because we as consumers who pay good money for their products, deserve better.

🔴 Paris Fashion Week : coup d’éclat devant le défilé @hermes ! Des « policières » surgissent en voiture 🚓🚨👮‍♀️ pancartes et slogans à l’appui :
📢 « Hermès : STOP aux peaux ‘exotiques’ » 
📢 « Tuer pour s’habiller : délit de cruauté »
Derrière les célèbres sacs « Birkin » et « Kelly » d’Hermès, qui peuvent coûter plus de 35 000 €, se cache une cruauté insoutenable. La peau des alligators sert à fabriquer des bracelets de montre, tandis que deux à trois crocodiles sont tués pour chaque sac à main.
🐊 Aucun accessoire de mode ne justifie une telle souffrance. Exigeons qu’Hermès renonce aux peaux « exotiques » !

RSPuram, Next to the banyan tree cross section.

Contact- 8056350379
Karuppusamy 

We stumbled upon him by the roadside, surrounded by colorful wires and years of quiet dedication. 
We showed him a Prada crochet bag and asked if he could make something like it. 
He glanced at it, smiled, and said, “It has a bit more design, but I can make it… maybe for ₹900–₹1000.”

We told him the original sells for ₹1,83,000. He looked up, wide-eyed — not out of jealousy or disbelief, but with a soft chuckle that said, “The world is strange.”

For over two decades, he and his family have been weaving beauty beneath the shade of a tree.

No brand name, no fancy showroom, just hands that know the rhythm of craft, and hearts full of quiet pride.

This is a story of value, not of price tags, but of patience, passion, and the kind of skill that’s passed down, not bought.

what’s in Jane Birkin’s Birkin?

In Agnès Varda’s dreamy 1988 portrait Jane B. par Agnès V., the legendary Jane Birkin literally empties her Hermes Birkin bag onto the steps of a Paris courtyard — a jumble of notes, makeup, pens and humble everyday objects spilling out as she looks at the camera with a wry smile. Rather than revealing secrets, Birkin playfully suggests that showing what’s in the bag reveals “very little,” turning a simple fashion moment into a meditation on identity, memory and the myths we build around style and celebrity.

The scene has since become a cultural touchstone — a proto “What’s in My Bag?” before that trend ever existed — and a reminder that the woman behind the name is far richer than any accessory could suggest.
Birkin’s life as an actress, singer, muse and icon, documented with intimacy and warmth by Varda, makes this moment less about luxury and more about the lived experience of a free spirit navigating art, aging, and self-definition.

🎥: Jane B. par Agnès V. 
  Directed by Agnès Varda in 1988 - France

Meet my dream bag ✨ After a lifetime of waiting, I get to do a #chanelunboxing of the bag I’ve wanted all along: a Chanel Classic Medium Double Flap in Beige Clair with caviar leather. I’ve been tucking away small amounts of money to put toward this bag for years. The bigger amount of savings went toward more important things first: our families, our new home, our wedding and other investments. Since I’m turning 30 in a few weeks and hit a lot of my business goals, I decided to invest in myself a bit and get this beauty! I know it’ll be a good investment since classic Chanel bags appreciate over time. I hope to one day be lucky enough to pass this bag down to my future daughter. A huge thank you to @juliaroseboston for helping me find the bag of my dreams since this bag is a bit of a unicorn. **Unraveling the Artistry Behind Togo Leather**

Today’s Artisan Spotlight delves into one of Hermès’ most beloved leathers, Togo. Known for its durability and lustrous finish, Togo is a favorite among collectors due to its ability to maintain shape while developing a beautiful patina over time.

Togo leather undergoes an intricate tanning process that enhances its natural resilience, making it ideal for high-wear items like Kelly bags or Birkins. The tanneries select the finest hides and apply a proprietary blend of oils and waxes to achieve Togo’s signature smooth texture and protective qualities.

For example, consider the classic Rouge Casaque Kelly 28 in Togo leather with Palladium hardware (PHW). As of March 2024, this particular piece retails for approximately $15,700. The enduring appeal of Togo lies not just in its practicality but also in its ability to age gracefully, reflecting the owner’s journey over time.

**The Ultimate Birthday Manifestation: The Birkin 25 Noir** 🖤

There is a certain magic that happens when preparation meets opportunity. As my birthday approached, I shared a simple wish with my SA: a Birkin 25 in Noir Togo—a piece that transcends seasons and trends. 

Today, that wish became a reality. Unboxing this beauty feels like the perfect culmination of a personal milestone. While some may chase perfection, I’ve fallen in love with the unique character of this Togo leather; its natural "neck lines" are a beautiful reminder that every piece of art carries its own soul and story. It isn’t just a bag; it’s a testament to a year of hard work and a celebration of what’s to come.

Louis Vuitton se complace en revelar su primera campaña protagonizada por Zendaya, la actriz ganadora del Globo de Oro, que debuta como la nueva Embajadora de la Casa Louis Vuitton y rostro del icónico bolso Capucines

Definida por los aires de ensueño de la Costa Azul, Zendaya posa en una secuencia idílica de imágenes fuertes y definidas, con una serie de modelos Capucines a cuestas, incluida una Capucines BB en miniatura, otra primicia de la campaña. 

Lanzado en 2013, el bolso Capucines debe su nombre a la Rue Neuve-des-Capucines, la calle de París donde Louis Vuitton abrió su primera tienda en 1854. 

Hoy en día, el bolso es uno de los diseños más emblemáticos de la Maison, una creación que viene desde la elegancia elaborada en piel Taurillon.

Past meets future, stitched in light. ✨ Presenting the CHANEL 26 (Cruise) Sequined Kelly Handle Bag — where the soul of a vintage icon is reborn through the Maison's most exquisite craftsmanship.

Fashion, they say, is a circle. And nothing proves it more beautifully than this. The legendary Kelly silhouette—beloved for its structured elegance and signature top handle—has been reimagined by Chanel for a new generation . The result? A piece that honors its archival roots while speaking the language of now.

What makes it extraordinary? The subtle subversion: beneath that classic handle flows a leather-woven chain strap , breaking tradition with an ease that feels utterly modern. Carry it by the handle for that polished, vintage-inspired moment, or wear it crossbody for an undone, effortlessly cool attitude . It's the duality that defines contemporary luxury.

Draped in iridescent (paillettes) , the entire surface catches light like scattered stardust—each tiny sequin adding depth, movement, and an almost magical shimmer . At a compact 13 x 19 x 7 cm, it's the perfect companion for evenings that demand attention, yet destined to be cherished for a lifetime . 🌟

KELLY BAG蛻變珠寶｜KELLYMORPHOSE

這大抵是最MINI的KELLY BAG！說的是愛馬仕的KELLYMORPHOSE珠寶系列，品牌珠寶創意總監PIERRE HARDY將KELLY BAG微縮蛻變成項鏈、手鐲、戒指等，以幽默玩味解構時代經典。

KELLY BAG的標誌細節如搭帶、旋轉袋扣、飾牌和鉚釘等，甚至是原個KELLY BAG，全都化身微型珠寶。當中最矚目的非SAC BIJOU KELLY莫屬，是由純銀鏈條製造的超迷你KELLY BAG，小巧可愛，卻不失實用，這「手袋」內居然可容納唇膏小物，是項鏈也是手袋；KELLY GAVROCHE將手袋搭帶轉化成鑲嵌1771顆鑽石的項圈，兩端挽成結，恍如繫結的飄逸絲巾，璀燦閃耀；KELLY BAG的標誌鎖扣也有不同詮釋，如KELLY GAVROCHE雙圈戒指、KELLY BAGUETTES手鐲及項圈等，巧妙的是鎖扣不只是裝飾，而是佩戴機關，轉動鎖扣才能開合，忠實還原設計初衷。PIERRE HARDY解釋：「纏繞的鏈條、一分為二的戒指、隨步履搖曳的匙扣──我喜歡動態，也喜歡以同樣流動的方式捕捉動感。我喜歡身體的律動，與流淌的創意彼此呼應。」將KELLY BAG戴上身，或許比挽著手袋更率性自由。

🎥 The New York Times

A year of quiet patience culminates in this singular moment. While some might call it the first acquisition of 2025, it feels more like the missing chapter of 2024 finally finding its rightful place in my collection.

The Kelly 25 in Étoupe is a masterclass in understated permanence. Though the world of Hermès offers an infinite spectrum of greys, Étoupe remains the "no-fail" anchor—a shade that adapts to the light and the life of the wearer. There is a beautiful unpredictability in Togo leather; each piece carries its own weight, its own grain, and its own soul. 

I find myself forever loyal to the *Retourne*—the inner stitch. It offers a certain slouchy, lived-in elegance that refuses to be too precious. It is sophisticated, yes, but more importantly, it is effortless. ✨

What is the one piece in your collection that feels less like an accessory and more like a second skin? 🕊️

#광고 보테가 베네타가 보여준 반전과 관능의 미학.

지난 2월 28일, 보테가 베네타가 밀라노에서 2026 겨울 컬렉션을 공개하며 예술적 대화를 시도했습니다. 이번 쇼에는 독보적인 존재감의 배우 윤여정과 스트레이 키즈 아이엔 등이 참석해 자리를 빛냈는데요. 이들은 쇼장 앞을 마비시키며 브랜드의 새로운 챕터를 함께 축하했습니다.

이번 컬렉션은 밀라노의 거리와 사람들의 태도에서 영감을 받아, 견고한 브루탈리즘 건축 양식과 부드러운 관능미를 절묘하게 결합했습니다. 특히 눈여겨볼 점은 바로 소재의 반전인데요. 퍼처럼 보이는 풍성한 텍스처가 사실은 실크와 니트를 정교하게 가공한 결과물이라는 점이 놀라움을 자아내죠. 피부 위에 또 하나의 피부를 입힌 듯한 장인 정신은 의류를 넘어 주얼리와 슈즈까지 이어지며 시각적 유희를 선사합니다.

마치 한 편의 오페라처럼 장엄하게 펼쳐진, 보테가 베네타가 제안하는 이번 시즌의 미학을 지금 바로 확인해 보세요.

Виконаний у глибоких аристократичних відтінках, він ідеально доповнить як розслаблений образ із денімом, так і витончену комбінацію зі спідницею максі. 🕊️

• Матеріал: Преміальна шкіра та замша з фірмовим тисненням.

• Розміри: S – XL (можливе замовлення спеціальних розмірів під ваші параметри)

米兰，粗粝与感性的对话在此展开。Bottega Veneta 2026冬季系列，是一个被柔化的结构主义季节，亦是一场关于亲密感与保护欲的深度研习。
冷峻外表下，隐藏着内在之美。经典日装被赋予深思熟虑的曲线，成为独属于穿着者的风格语言。精准的线条让位于张扬的姿态——这是一场跨越性别与代际的对话，也是一抹怀旧的花香：祖母的晚宴手包，父亲的旧鞋。
匠艺肌理层层铺展：丝绸、剪花织物、针织面料与科技纤维摹刻出皮毛质感，在服装、珠宝与鞋履间灵动漾开。随着简约向歌剧风格的演变，玛丽亚·卡拉斯与帕索里尼的身影隐约浮现——他们的大胆先锋与不羁爱情，成为本季的精神注脚。
在歌剧院、剧院、公共广场，米兰人盛装打扮——为自己，也为所属群体。用心，且信心十足。
这即是集体精神的表达：心灵、思想与双手的奇妙协作。

LOUISE TROTTER APPOINTED CREATIVE DIRECTOR OF BOTTEGA VENETA

Bottega Veneta and Kering are delighted to announce the appointment of Louise Trotter as the new Creative Director for the House, marking the opening of an exciting new chapter in the brand’s creative journey.

Louise Trotter, Creative Director of Carven, is celebrated for her ability to draw inspiration from real life and meticulous approach to craftsmanship. She will join Bottega Veneta at the end of January 2025.

Copenhagen Fashion Week might officially be our new favorite for bag spotting!  All the images are now up on our site and Substack (don’t sleep on it, we saved some gems just for there). Here are five I can’t stop thinking about:

1. This @loewe Puzzle bag + fire-engine-red nails = the color/texture combo that has me in a chokehold. I’m not free, and I don’t want to be.

2. A black-and-white striped Chanel 22 I’ve never seen in the wild before?! Yes, please. And paired with Chanel sunnies? The audacity. The excellence.

3. Forget minis, this @balenciaga is going full vintage and unapologetically oversized. Big bag energy only, especially in a bright color!

4. Remember @gucci x Adidas? Neither did I… until this bag came strutting by, reminding me that extra is still everything.

5. This gradient jacket (tan melting into green) next to a silver Chanel 25? Yeah, I’m spiraling. Clearly, the universe is telling me I *need* a 25.

CHANEL’s cross-town line–presented in New York, Matthieu Blazy’s first Métiers d’art collection features a joyful cavalcade of personalities, seen through a filmic lens. Art Deco extravagances, elegant tweeds pushing the boundaries of savoir-faire, sophisticated embroideries and fantastic animal prints, the collection shifts through time and space.

The Métiers d’art collections are unique to CHANEL. Every year since 2002, they celebrate the exceptional French savoir-faire at the heart of the House’s creations. This annual rendezvous is a declaration of love to the artisans who contribute to CHANEL collections all year round.
